behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-signature-verifier-to-queue wiring: reject path must both set 401 and prevent enqueue side effect. Unit tests of pure HMAC logic miss miswired control flow. Rejected higher level: no behavior text requires real network/OS/subprocess or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with real middleware/body extraction and real signature verification module; send a mismatched signed payload; assert 401 and zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering/hand-off guarantee across modules (handler and persistent queue). The real failure is missing await/transaction boundary before ack. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/OS primitives beyond local persistence; local DB/filesystem-backed queue is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: With real queue implementation (local persistent backend), trigger a valid request and instrument timing/order so the test proves enqueue commit completes before response completion.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly about observable HTTP response timing versus asynchronous downstream completion. Mocked/in-process-only call paths can hide real server/event-loop response behavior; real HTTP interaction is the failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service, make a real HTTP request while downstream worker intentionally blocks/sleeps, assert response is returned before worker completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a wall-clock SLO under concurrency and backlog pressure, which depends on runtime scheduling and real HTTP serving behavior. Mocks typically hide queue contention and timing regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service with concurrent slow downstream jobs already in flight; send new webhook(s) over HTTP; measure end-to-end latency and assert each response <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at state handoff boundary (handler + persistent queue/idempotency store). Unit logic can miss real duplicate-write races or persistence-constraint mismatches. Rejected System: no required real network/OS beyond local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Using real persistence-backed queue/idempotency path, submit two valid requests with same event ID; assert only one durable queue entry exists.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interface: queue-full signal must map to specific HTTP status/header. A unit test of mapping alone is weaker because real adapter/exception translation can break. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network conditions, only correct inter-module propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to real queue adapter configured at capacity (or forced full state) and verify response is 503 with valid Retry-After and no accepted enqueue.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates queue module + persistence backend behavior under configured limits; pure unit fakes can hide backend/state bugs. Rejected System: no subprocess/external API/real network requirement is stated; local persistent backend is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small capacity, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more and assert deterministic rejection plus unchanged stored depth.
